## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Sibling pace to BBAAJ. Implements credentialed cases 4 (retriever) and 5 (director) of pristine-lifecycle as theurge cases mirroring the operator's flow through `rbw-aC` / `rbw-aK` plus access-probe verification.
  
  The character of this work is mechanical — no design conversation should be needed at implementation time. Implementation specifics surface in real-time conversation; this docket holds intent only, not pre-staged choices.
  
  ## Scope
  
  Cases 4 and 5 each: levy own throwaway depot, install post-levy rbrr fields, mantle throwaway governor, charter/knight role SA, stage RBRA through assay, run access-probe, forfeit role SA, forfeit governor, unmake depot.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor the paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on very first failure cleanly and clearly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
  - Reuse BBAAJ-locked patterns from the paddock: throwaway-prefix install, fact-file invocation, edit-and-commit for rbrr field installation, section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.
  - Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB, BBAAJ).
  - Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
  - Recovery from mid-run failures beyond `rbw-MZ` + retry.
  - Any test-orthogonality affordances that would conflict with wall-clock budget.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Design decisions
10	
11	### Single operator prerequisite chain
12	
13	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
14	
15	### Entry contract — load-bearing
16	
17	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
18	- Working tree clean
19	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
20	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
21	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
22	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
23	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
24	
25	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
26	
27	### Pristine-lifecycle fixture architecture
28	
29	The marshal-zero gate AND the SA/depot lifecycle exercises live as cases inside one new theurge fixture (`pristine-lifecycle`):
30	
31	| # | Case | Idempotent? | Cost |
32	|---|------|-------------|------|
33	| 1 | `marshal-zero-attestation` | N/A — gate | Free |
34	| 2 | `depot-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (soft-delete graveyard accepted) |
35	| 3 | `governor-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (case levies its own throwaway depot to satisfy `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` precondition; depot is unmade in case cleanup) |
36	| 4 | `retriever-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + retriever) | Free + light read probe |
37	| 5 | `director-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + director) | Light Cloud Build / GAR write probe |
38	
39	Cases 4 and 5 each create their own throwaway governor (single-case independence is load-bearing); after the case, both throwaway SAs are forfeited. Cases 2-5 are idempotent against existing canonical state — they can run while a canonical depot/SA chain exists, against any state.
40	
41	**Single-case run** (e.g., `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh depot-lifecycle`) skips case 1's gate and exercises just the named case. **Full-fixture run** goes through case 1 first; case 1 gates the rest.
42	
43	### Canonical infrastructure setup is a separate phase
44	
45	After the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes, `rbw-tP` proceeds to canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC): mantle real governor + deploy real RBRA, charter real retriever + deploy RBRA, knight real director + deploy RBRA, levy canonical depot. This persistent state is what reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs depend on.
46	
47	Two depots created per pristine run: one throwaway (case 2 of fixture) + one canonical (BBAAC). Both quota-bearing. Case 3 also levies+unmakes its own throwaway depot — counted as a third quota-bearing event but cleaned up within the case.
48	
49	### Failure mode contract
50	
51	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
52	
53	### Release-branch execution contract
54	
55	Three properties bind every case in this fixture. They shape how a run is shaped — distinct from, and constraining, the failure-mode contract above.
56	
57	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
58	
59	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each fixture step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that. Minimizing fixture commits is anti-goal.
60	
61	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A case fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches, no "cleanup so the next case can run." Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. The operator never asks "should I continue past this failure?" — the answer is always no.
62	
63	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance for graceful continuation, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy tee files — is non-load-bearing complexity and does not belong in pristine-lifecycle code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
64	
65	### Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)
66	
67	Durable choices binding all pristine-lifecycle case implementations (BBAAB, BBAAJ, BBAAK, and future BBAAC vessel-commit cases).
68	
69	**Throwaway-prefix mechanism: edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + git commit.** Cases 2-5 need non-blank `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`/`RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` to invoke depot/SA tabtargets. The fixture mimics the human workflow: open rbrr.env, edit, `git add` + `git commit`. After the fixture runs, HEAD has walked off marshal-zero — recovery is `rbw-MZ`, matching the "start-over-from-zero" failure mode above.
70	
71	Rejected alternative — env-var override: `rbrr_cli.sh` kindle does `source rbrr_regime.sh; source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}; zrbrr_kindle; zrbrr_enforce` — sourcing rbrr.env overwrites any pre-set env, then enforce locks `readonly`. Pre-set env is silently discarded. Confirmed by tracing.
72	
73	Rejected alternative — in-memory restore (RAII): mutates rbrr.env without committing, leaves a transient dirty-tree window where a crash or sibling tooling sees uncommitted state that isn't operator-authored. Edit-and-commit is honest.
74	
75	**Idempotent install helper.** A shared function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` reads current rbrr.env values; if blank (post-marshal-zero), writes throwaway values and commits; if already throwaway, no-op. Cases 2-5 call this as their first step. One commit per fixture run, not per case.
76	
77	**Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. Case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (named distinctly from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01` vs `pristq01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and commits, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes the depot in cleanup. The same pattern (commit a small targeted edit, run, clean up) is the model for future cases that need additional rbrr/rbrv/rbrn fields populated.
78	
79	**Tabtarget invocation style — fact files over stdout parsing.** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (depot project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. The BURV-isolated invoke also makes invoke counters and per-call output capture available to the case. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts (e.g., `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` cases) keep the raw `Command::new(&tt).current_dir(&root)` style. For imprint-channel tabtargets like `rbtd-ap` (cases 4-5), use `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` with the role as imprint.
80	
81	**Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**
82	- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case 1 only (BBAAB-landed)
83	- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
84	- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)
85	
86	`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.
87	
88	**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.
89	
90	**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.
91	
92	### Tier layering
93	
94	Three tiers, escalating cost:
95	
96	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
97	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
98	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
99	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
100	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
101	
102	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
103	
104	### Operator scope
105	
106	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).
107	
108	### Coupling to ₣A_
109	
110	This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.
111	
112	## References
113	
114	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
115	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
116	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
117	- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
118	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
119	- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
120	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
121	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
122	- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice
123	
124	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAK
125	
126	## Character
127	
128	Sibling pace to BBAAJ. Implements credentialed cases 4 (retriever) and 5 (director) of pristine-lifecycle as theurge cases mirroring the operator's flow through `rbw-aC` / `rbw-aK` plus access-probe verification.
129	
130	The character of this work is mechanical — no design conversation should be needed at implementation time. Implementation specifics surface in real-time conversation; this docket holds intent only, not pre-staged choices.
131	
132	## Scope
133	
134	Cases 4 and 5 each: levy own throwaway depot, install post-levy rbrr fields, mantle throwaway governor, charter/knight role SA, stage RBRA through assay, run access-probe, forfeit role SA, forfeit governor, unmake depot.
135	
136	## Constraints
137	
138	- Honor the paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on very first failure cleanly and clearly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
139	- Reuse BBAAJ-locked patterns from the paddock: throwaway-prefix install, fact-file invocation, edit-and-commit for rbrr field installation, section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.
140	- Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.
141	
142	## Out of scope
143	
144	- Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB, BBAAJ).
145	- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
146	- Recovery from mid-run failures beyond `rbw-MZ` + retry.
147	- Any test-orthogonality affordances that would conflict with wall-clock budget.

After the fixture runs, HEAD has walked off marshal-zero — recovery is `rbw-MZ`, matching the "start-over-from-zero" failure mode above.

Rejected alternative — env-var override: `rbrr_cli.sh` kindle does `source rbrr_regime.sh; source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}; zrbrr_kindle; zrbrr_enforce` — sourcing rbrr.env overwrites any pre-set env, then enforce locks `readonly`. Pre-set env is silently discarded. Confirmed by tracing.

Rejected alternative — in-memory restore (RAII): mutates rbrr.env without committing, leaves a transient dirty-tree window where a crash or sibling tooling sees uncommitted state that isn't operator-authored. Edit-and-commit is honest.

**Idempotent install helper.** A shared function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` reads current rbrr.env values; if blank (post-marshal-zero), writes throwaway values and commits; if already throwaway, no-op. Cases 2-5 call this as their first step. One commit per fixture run, not per case.

**Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. Case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (named distinctly from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01` vs `pristq01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and commits, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes the depot in cleanup. The same pattern (commit a small targeted edit, run, clean up) is the model for future cases that need additional rbrr/rbrv/rbrn fields populated.

**Tabtarget invocation style — fact files over stdout parsing.** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (depot project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. The BURV-isolated invoke also makes invoke counters and per-call output capture available to the case. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts (e.g., `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` cases) keep the raw `Command::new(&tt).current_dir(&root)` style. For imprint-channel tabtargets like `rbtd-ap` (cases 4-5), use `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` with the role as imprint.

**Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**
- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case 1 only (BBAAB-landed)
- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)

`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.

**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.

**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.

### Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

### Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).

### Coupling to ₣A_

This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAK

## Character

Sibling pace to BBAAJ. Implements credentialed cases 4 (retriever) and 5 (director) of pristine-lifecycle as theurge cases mirroring the operator's flow through `rbw-aC` / `rbw-aK` plus access-probe verification.

The character of this work is mechanical — no design conversation should be needed at implementation time. Implementation specifics surface in real-time conversation; this docket holds intent only, not pre-staged choices.

## Scope

Cases 4 and 5 each: levy own throwaway depot, install post-levy rbrr fields, mantle throwaway governor, charter/knight role SA, stage RBRA through assay, run access-probe, forfeit role SA, forfeit governor, unmake depot.

## Constraints

- Honor the paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on very first failure cleanly and clearly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
- Reuse BBAAJ-locked patterns from the paddock: throwaway-prefix install, fact-file invocation, edit-and-commit for rbrr field installation, section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.
- Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.

## Out of scope

- Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB, BBAAJ).
- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
- Recovery from mid-run failures beyond `rbw-MZ` + retry.
- Any test-orthogonality affordances that would conflict with wall-clock budget.

content.text:
Mounted ₣BB. Oriented.

## Heat: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) — 4/11

Building `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a release tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero was just committed, so silent-first-build bugs get caught by construction. The pristine-lifecycle fixture has five cases:

| # | Case | Status |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | marshal-zero-attestation | landed (BBAAB) |
| 2 | depot-lifecycle | landed (BBAAJ) |
| 3 | governor-lifecycle | landed (BBAAJ) |
| 4 | retriever-lifecycle | **next (BBAAK)** |
| 5 | director-lifecycle | **next (BBAAK)** |

## Next pace: ₢BBAAK phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed [rough]

**The shape of where we are.** BBAAK was attempted once already (commit `fcf96f0c` "Step 1: rbrr-post-levy completion") and **reverted** (commit `51f4e30f`) — the lesson logged in the revert message is that the BBAAK docket had pre-engineered scope (added two fact files, renamed the install helper to `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` taking three fields, etc.) that locked premature choices for cases 4-5 before the release-machinery contract was clear. After the revert the paddock got curried to add the **Release-branch execution contract** (stop on first failure / commits as orientation / branch execution), and the BBAAK docket got stripped down to intent-only.

So this restart is intentionally cold: the docket says "Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude." The shape per case is fixed (levy throwaway depot → install post-levy rbrr → mantle throwaway governor → charter/knight role SA → stage RBRA through assay → access-probe → forfeit role SA → forfeit governor → unmake depot) but every implementation choice underneath is open.

## Things I think we need to actually decide before any code lands

1. **Which post-levy rbrr fields do cases 4-5 actually need.** The reverted Step 1 assumed all three (DEPOT_PROJECT_ID + GAR_REPOSITORY + GCB_POOL_STEM). DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is clearly needed for governor-mantle. GAR_REPOSITORY and GCB_POOL_STEM may or may not be load-bearing for retriever-charter and director-knight respectively — that's an empirical question, not a docket decision. **Approach:** start with DEPOT_PROJECT_ID only; let `zrbrr_enforce` surface what else is required when we hit the wall.

2. **What "stage RBRA through assay" means concretely.** Is this `rbgv_AccessProbe` (JWT for retriever, OAuth for director)? Is it a separate handbook-shaped step that drops the key file at the canonical RBRA path, then runs the probe? The handbook tracks (`rbhocd_credential_director.sh`, `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh`) describe the operator flow — case 4/5 mirror those. We should walk the handbook for one role end-to-end before opening a Rust file.

3. **Granularity of section §3.** Paddock locks §3 as `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` covering cases 4-5. Open: does each case carry its own throwaway-governor lifecycle (so case 4 mantles+forfeits its own governor, case 5 mantles+forfeits its own), or does §3 hold one shared throwaway governor across both cases? The docket says "each: ... mantle throwaway governor, ... forfeit governor" — that reads as per-case. Confirms with paddock single-case independence is load-bearing.

4. **Commit cadence inside a case.** Paddock says commits-during-run are first-class. Open: every rbrr edit gets its own commit (more anchors, longer history) vs. one commit per case (less noise). The BBAAJ pattern was one commit per rbrr field-install per case. Following BBAAJ verbatim is the safe default.

5. **Whether cases 4 and 5 should land in one notch or two.** They're sibling cases with parallel structure — landing them together rhymes with BBAAJ's two-case landing. But each is a substantial chunk and the revert lesson suggests preferring smaller increments under real-time review.

## Recommendation

Pick one of items 2-5 to actually discuss now, decide it, then we surface item 1 against running code rather than against the docket. Item 2 (handbook walk for retriever/director credential staging) is the highest-leverage starting point — it grounds the rest in operator-visible reality and avoids re-locking choices in the abstract.

Where would you like to start?

    "${z_instance}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Retriever SA"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Artifact Registry Reader role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Artifact Registry Reader"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "retriever-reader"

  buc_step 'Adding Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                              \
    "${z_token}"                                         \
    "Grant Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"                           \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER}"  \

    "retriever-analysis"

  local z_actual_rbra_file="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_instance}.rbra"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_actual_rbra_file}"

rbgg_knight_director() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_instance="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Knight Director service account instance"
  buc_doc_param "instance" "Instance name (required)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_instance}"     || buc_die "Instance name required"
  zburd_sentinel
  test -d "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" || buc_die "BURD_OUTPUT_DIR does not exist: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_director}-${z_instance}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Knighting Director service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                      \
    "${z_account_name}"                                    \
    "Recipe Bottle Director (${z_instance})"               \
    "Create/destroy container images for ${z_instance}"    \
    "${z_instance}" "${RBCC_role_director}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Director SA"

  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Cloud Build Editor role (project scope)'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Cloud Build Editor"              \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR}" \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-cb"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Project Viewer"                  \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/viewer"                          \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-viewer"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Worker Pool User"               \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser"       \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-pool"

  buc_step 'Grant serviceAccountUser on Mason'
  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "${z_account_email}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser"

  buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  local z_gar_prop_delay=3
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=420
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""
  local z_gar_get_err=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry: newly-created Director SA may not be visible yet
    if test "${z_gar_get_code}" = "400"; then
      z_gar_get_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_err=""
      case "${z_gar_get_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

rbgg_forfeit_service_account() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_sa_email="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Forfeit a service account"
  buc_doc_param "email" "Email address of the service account to forfeit"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_sa_email}" || buc_die "Service account email required"

  buc_step "Forfeiting service account: ${z_sa_email}"

  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}" \

  buc_success "Forfeit operation completed"
}

zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_role="${1}"

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor)
      test -n "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    director)
      test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    retriever)
      test -n "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown role '${z_role}': expected governor | director | retriever"
      ;;
  esac
}

# HTTP GET with bounded exponential-backoff retry on transient 5xx responses.
# On any non-5xx response (including auth errors), populates z_code_file and returns.
# On curl-network failure or exhausted 5xx retries, buc_die. The caller evaluates
# the final HTTP status via its own case-switch on the populated z_code_file.
zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local -r z_label="${1}"
  local -r z_url="${2}"
  local -r z_token="${3}"
  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"

  local z_attempt=1
  local z_delay="${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
  local z_curl_status=0
  local z_code=""

  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; do
    buc_log_args "${z_label}: HTTP GET attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"

    z_curl_status=0
    curl                                                     \
        -sS                                                  \
        -X GET                                               \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"                \
        -H "Accept: application/json"                        \
        -o "${z_resp_file}"                                  \
        -w "%{http_code}"                                    \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"               \
        "${z_url}" > "${z_code_file}"                        \
                   2> "${z_stderr_file}"                     \
      || z_curl_status=$?

    buc_log_args "${z_label}: curl exit status ${z_curl_status}"
    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"

    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: curl failed (network/SSL/DNS) — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "${z_label}: failed to read HTTP code file"
    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "${z_label}: empty HTTP code from curl"

    case "${z_code}" in
      500|502|503|504)
        if test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; then
          buc_step "${z_label}: transient HTTP ${z_code}, retrying in ${z_delay}s (attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
          sleep "${z_delay}"
          z_delay=$(( z_delay * 2 ))
          z_attempt=$(( z_attempt + 1 ))
        else
          buc_die "${z_label}: repeated transient HTTP ${z_code} after ${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts — see ${z_resp_file}"
        fi
        ;;
      *)
        return 0
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

# Execute one Artifact Registry packages.list probe iteration for a JWT SA role.
# Writes response to ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE, HTTP code to ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE,
# and stderr to ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE (kindle-constant paths for forensic visibility).
zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_role="${1}"
  local z_iteration="${2}"

  buc_log_args "JWT SA probe iteration ${z_iteration} for role '${z_role}'"

  buc_log_args "Resolve RBRA file for role"
  local z_rbra_file
  z_rbra_file=$(zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture "${z_role}") || return 1

  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "RBRA file not found for role ${z_role}: ${z_rbra_file}"

  buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${z_rbra_file}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to obtain OAuth token for role ${z_role} (iteration ${z_iteration})"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty OAuth token for role ${z_role} (iteration ${z_iteration})"

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.SingleCase.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_case="${1:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}" ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

rbte_probe() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_role="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "No role imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.governor.sh)"

  local z_iterations=5
  local z_delay_ms=1500

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor|director|retriever)
      buc_step "JWT SA access probe: ${z_role}"
      rbgv_jwt_sa_probe "${z_role}" "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "${z_role} JWT access probe passed"
      ;;
    payor)
      buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe"
      source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
      zrbrp_kindle
      zrbrp_enforce
      rbgv_payor_oauth_probe "${z_iterations}" "${z_delay_ms}"
      buc_success "Payor OAuth access probe passed"
      ;;
    *)

zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_role="${1}"
  local z_iteration="${2}"

  buc_log_args "JWT SA probe iteration ${z_iteration} for role '${z_role}'"

  buc_log_args "Resolve RBRA file for role"
  local z_rbra_file
  z_rbra_file=$(zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture "${z_role}") || return 1

  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "RBRA file not found for role ${z_role}: ${z_rbra_file}"

  buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${z_rbra_file}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to obtain OAuth token for role ${z_role} (iteration ${z_iteration})"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty OAuth token for role ${z_role} (iteration ${z_iteration})"

  buc_log_args "Build Artifact Registry packages.list URL"
  test -n "${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_GCP_REGION:-}"       || buc_die "RBRR_GCP_REGION is not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY:-}"   || buc_die "RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY is not set"

  local -r z_ar_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}/repositories/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/packages"
  local -r z_ar_label="JWT SA probe [${z_role}] iteration ${z_iteration}"

  buc_log_args "Call Artifact Registry packages.list (with transient-5xx retry)"
  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
    "${z_ar_label}"             \
    "${z_ar_url}"               \
    "${z_token}"                \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}"     \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}"     \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}"

  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read AR HTTP code file"
  test -n "${z_code}"                || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from AR curl"

  buc_log_args "AR packages.list HTTP ${z_code} for role ${z_role} iteration ${z_iteration}"

  case "${z_code}" in
    200|206)
      buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] iteration ${z_iteration}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    401|403)
      buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] iteration ${z_iteration}: access denied (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    *)
      local z_err=""
      if jq -e . "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_err=$(jq -r '.error.message // "Unknown error"' "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null) || z_err="Unknown error"
      else
        z_err="Non-JSON response (HTTP ${z_code})"
      fi
      buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] iteration ${z_iteration}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
      ;;
  esac
}

# Execute one CRM projects.get probe iteration for the Payor OAuth flow.
# Writes response to ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE, HTTP code to ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE,
# and stderr to ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE (kindle-constant paths for forensic visibility).
zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_iteration="${1}"

  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"

  buc_log_args "Authenticate via Payor OAuth refresh token flow"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) \
    || buc_die "Failed to obtain Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"

  buc_log_args "Build CRM projects.get URL"
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"

  local -r z_crm_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}"
  local -r z_crm_label="Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"

  buc_log_args "Call CRM projects.get on payor project (with transient-5xx retry)"
  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
    "${z_crm_label}"            \
    "${z_crm_url}"              \
    "${z_token}"                \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}"    \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}"    \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE}"

  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read CRM HTTP code file"
  test -n "${z_code}"                 || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from CRM curl"

  buc_log_args "CRM projects.get HTTP ${z_code} for Payor iteration ${z_iteration}"

  case "${z_code}" in
    200)
      buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    401|403)
      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: access denied (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    *)
      local z_err=""
      if jq -e . "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_err=$(jq -r '.error.message // "Unknown error"' "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null) || z_err="Unknown error"
      else
        z_err="Non-JSON response (HTTP ${z_code})"
      fi
      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
      ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgv_*)

# Probe 1: JWT SA Access Probe

#   2. Call Artifact Registry packages.list to verify token grants read access
#   3. Sleep for configured delay

Read the existing case 3 (governor-lifecycle), the `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` bodies, and the access-probe machinery. Now I can give you the precise step lists.

## Empirical preconditions (from the live code)

The reverted Step 1 instinct was partially right and partially wrong. The actual load-bearing facts:

| Field | Why it's needed | Cases |
|-------|-----------------|-------|
| `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` | governor-mantle precondition; access-probe URL | 4, 5 |
| `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` | director-knight reads it (`rbgg_Governor.sh:582`); JWT AR probe URL (`rbgv_AccessProbe.sh:190`) | 4, 5 |
| `RBRR_GCP_REGION` | JWT AR probe URL | 4, 5 — but **not blanked by marshal-zero** |
| `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` | Only matters if Cloud Build is invoked; access-probe doesn't | **neither** |

So the reverted helper's three-field shape was over-specified; two fields suffice. (Confirms the revert lesson — pre-engineering locked a wrong scope.)

## Case 4 — retriever-lifecycle

| # | Step | Tabtarget / helper | Fact captured |
|---|------|--------------------|---------------|
| 1 | Install throwaway prefixes (idempotent edit+commit) | `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` *(exists)* | — |
| 2 | Levy throwaway depot (moniker `pristr01`) | `rbw-dL` (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY`) | `RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` |
| 3 | Install `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` + `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` (edit+commit) | new helper, two-field shape | — |
| 4 | Mantle throwaway governor | `rbw-aM` (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE`) | `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL` |
| 5 | Charter retriever (instance = `pristr01`) | `rbw-aC` *(new colophon `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_RET_CHARTER`)* | retriever SA email + `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/pristr01.rbra` |
| 6 | Stage RBRA at `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (`cp` + `mkdir -p` parent) | filesystem | — |
| 7 | Run access-probe | `rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.retriever.sh` (imprint channel via `rbtdri_find_tabtarget`) | — |
| 8 | Remove staged RBRA file | filesystem | — |
| 9 | Forfeit retriever SA | `rbw-aF` (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_FORFEIT`) with retriever email | — |
| 10 | Forfeit governor SA | `rbw-aF` with governor email | — |
| 11 | Unmake throwaway depot | `rbw-dU` with confirm-skip | — |

## Case 5 — director-lifecycle

Identical structure; deltas only:

| # | Step | Difference from case 4 |
|---|------|------------------------|
| 2 | Levy throwaway depot | moniker `pristd01` |
| 5 | Knight director | `rbw-aK` *(new colophon `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DIR_KNIGHT`)*, instance `pristd01` |
| 6 | Stage RBRA | path `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` |
| 7 | Run access-probe | `rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.director.sh` |
| 9 | Forfeit role SA | director SA email |

## What needs minting / new code

- 2 new manifest colophon consts: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_RET_CHARTER` (`rbw-aC`), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DIR_KNIGHT` (`rbw-aK`); both added to the pristine-lifecycle required list.
- 1 new helper `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` — two-field shape (DEPOT_PROJECT_ID + GAR_REPOSITORY), single edit-and-commit. *(Smaller than the reverted three-field version.)*
- 2 new fact-file basenames in `rbgc_Constants.sh`: `RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` only (no GCB_POOL_STEM); + matching emit in `rbgp_depot_levy`. *(One of the two new fact-files from the reverted Step 1 — the load-bearing one.)*
- 2 new monikers + 2 case impl funcs in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs` (`rbtdrp_retriever_lifecycle`, `rbtdrp_director_lifecycle`).
- New section §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` registered in `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.
- Fact-file emit in `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` so cases can capture the role SA email for forfeit (currently those functions only print install hints).

## Open question for you before I write any code

The cases each need to capture the **role SA email** (retriever / director) to pass to forfeit at step 9. The current `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` only print install hints, no fact-write. Two options:

- **A.** Add `buf_write_fact RBGP_FACT_RETRIEVER_SA_EMAIL` / `RBGP_FACT_DIRECTOR_SA_EMAIL` inside `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` at the value-known site (`z_account_email`).
- **B.** Reconstruct the email in Rust via `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${instance}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}` — but this duplicates the formula and pulls a const dependency through.

A is the BBAAJ-shaped choice (fact emission at the value-known site). Confirm A and I'll proceed; or push back if you see something I'm missing.

content.text:
## Where `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` comes from

It's **derived inside `rbgp_depot_levy` from the depot moniker** (the first argument to `rbw-dL`):

```
rbgp_Payor.sh:571   z_depot_name = $1                        # the moniker, e.g. "pristr01"
rbgp_Payor.sh:769   z_repository_name = "rbw-${z_depot_name}-repository"
rbgp_Payor.sh:681   z_pool_stem       = "rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
```

The levy **creates** the GAR repository under that name as part of its work, then **prints** these values for the operator at the end (`rbgp_Payor.sh:853-857`):

```
buc_info "Update RBRR configuration:"
buc_bare "  RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=${z_depot_project_id}"
buc_bare "  RBRR_GCP_REGION=${z_region}"
buc_bare "  RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=${z_repository_name}"
buc_bare "  RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM=${z_pool_stem}"
```

**Normal human flow:** operator runs `rbw-dL <moniker>` → reads the printed banner → manually edits `.rbk/rbrr.env` to set those four fields → continues to `rbw-aM` (mantle governor).

So the "proper setting" is a **deterministic function of the moniker the operator chose** — `rbw-${moniker}-repository`. There's no external source of truth; the levy *is* the source of truth.

## What this means for our fixture

The pristine-lifecycle fixture is mechanizing the human banner-read step. Two options:

- **A. Read it from a fact file** (BBAAJ pattern). Add `buf_write_fact RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY ${z_repository_name}` inside `rbgp_depot_levy` at the value-known site (line ~769). Case captures via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. *Same shape as the reverted Step 1, just narrower (one fact, not two).*
- **B. Compute it in Rust from the moniker.** `format!("rbw-{}-repository", moniker)`. Trivial, no shell-side change. But duplicates the `"rbw-{}-repository"` formula across two language boundaries — a maintenance hazard if the levy ever changes its derivation.

A is the BBAAJ-aligned choice — fact-file emission keeps Rust ignorant of the formula. The cost is small: one new const in `rbgc_Constants.sh`, one `buf_write_fact` call in `rbgp_depot_levy`, one matching linked term in RBS0 Payor Fact Files section.

(Note: `RBRR_GCP_REGION` is **not** blanked by marshal-zero — it lives in the operator's hand-curated baseline rbrr.env, not in the post-levy facts. So the helper's "post-levy install" only needs to write the two fields the levy actually computes: `DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` + `GAR_REPOSITORY`. `GCB_POOL_STEM` would be a third if anything we care about needed it, but for cases 4-5 nothing does.)

Confirm A and the moniker is the right knob, and I'll proceed.

content.text:
## Yes — and the redundancy is structural, not accidental

The depot levy creates `z_depot_project_id` with this format (`rbgp_Payor.sh:618`):

```
${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}
```

So the moniker is **embedded inside DEPOT_PROJECT_ID**, between the prefix-type and the timestamp. And `rbgp_depot_unmake` already proves the system can extract it back out — `rbgp_Payor.sh:940-943` does exactly that:

```
z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"
z_depot_name="${z_without_prefix:0:$((z_name_len - z_ts_suffix_len))}"
```

So:

| Field | Provenance | Redundant with |
|-------|------------|----------------|
| `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` | levy mints it | (primary) |
| `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` | `rbw-${moniker}-repository` | DEPOT_PROJECT_ID |
| `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` | `rbw-${moniker}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}` | DEPOT_PROJECT_ID |
| `RBRR_GCP_REGION` | operator-curated, not levy-derived | (independent) |

**Two of the four post-levy RBRR fields are denormalized projections of DEPOT_PROJECT_ID.** The system already round-trips through this projection inside `rbgp_depot_unmake` — there's no theoretical objection to deriving the same way for read paths.

## What this means for the work

This is real. But it's bigger than BBAAK. The choice tree:

1. **Accept the redundancy for BBAAK.** Mechanize what the operator does today. Add the GAR_REPOSITORY fact, install both fields. Land cases 4-5. Open an itch (or scar) noting that GAR_REPOSITORY/GCB_POOL_STEM are derivable and should probably collapse into derived constants reading DEPOT_PROJECT_ID. Future heat eliminates the redundancy and removes the storage.

2. **Eliminate the redundancy first.** Make GAR_REPOSITORY and GCB_POOL_STEM into RBDC-derived constants (computed once at kindle from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID). Marshal-zero stops blanking them because they no longer exist as RBRR fields. Cases 4-5 then need only DEPOT_PROJECT_ID installed post-levy — back to the original BBAAJ helper shape. Lands cleaner code, but it's a much larger structural change touching every reader of those two RBRR fields, the spec, the operator banner, the regime tooling, and probably has subtle implications I haven't traced.

## Recommendation

Option 1 for BBAAK, with the redundancy captured as an itch (or, if it's well-understood enough, as a scar with the diagnosis preserved). The pristine-lifecycle fixture's job is to expose first-build assumptions; eliminating denormalization is a separate piece of design work — there may be reasons the redundancy was introduced (operator override capability? historical migration?) that warrant a deliberate look rather than incidental cleanup riding along on cases 4-5.

This is also exactly the kind of finding the paddock's *commits-during-the-run-are-first-class* + *real-time review* posture is designed to surface. Pre-engineering would have shipped Option 1 silently; the conversation surfaced the underlying observation. Worth marking — even if we don't act on it now.

Want me to draft the itch text, or proceed with Option 1 and let you decide separately?

content.text:
The manifold is *almost* clear. Let me reflect what I think you're saying, then mark the places where I see ambiguity.

## What I think you're proposing

**One identity per depot, two RBRR fields.** RBRR retains:
- `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` — operator namespace (e.g., `prlc-`)
- `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` — short name within namespace (e.g., `alpha`, `pristr01`)

Everything else becomes RBDC-derived:
- project_id = `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${DEPOT_MONIKER}` (or `${CLOUD_PREFIX}-depot-${MONIKER}`, see below)
- gar_repository = `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-repository` (or similar)
- gcb_pool_stem = `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- mason_email = `${CLOUD_PREFIX}mason-${MONIKER}@...` (or the existing `mason-${moniker}` shape)

**Uniqueness contract shifts to the operator.** Levy refuses to create when any derived resource exists; the operator picks a fresh moniker rather than the levy hiding a timestamp.

**`RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` subsumes today's `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` constant and the hard-coded `rbw-` in derived-name formulas.** Three prefix concepts collapse into one.

## What I think is clear and well-shaped

- The redundancy goes away cleanly. RBRR holds intent (namespace + name), code derives the rest.
- Derived names become *uniformly* operator-namespaced. Today's mix (`rb-depot-` for project, `rbw-` for repo/pool, `mason-` for SA) becomes one prefix throughout.
- The unmake-side moniker extraction (`rbgp_Payor.sh:940-943`) becomes trivial — it's already in RBRR.
- The "fact-file emission for GAR_REPOSITORY" question evaporates. There's nothing to emit; it's derivable.

## What I see as not yet clear

**1. Soft-delete window vs. test-fixture cadence.** GCP project IDs are globally unique and reserved 30 days after deletion. Today the timestamp makes every levy unique. Without it: the operator using a stable moniker like `alpha` accepts a 30-day wait if they ever destroy-and-recreate. **But the pristine-lifecycle fixture destroys and recreates on every run.** That means either:
- The fixture rotates monikers per run (effectively re-introducing a timestamp, just into the moniker — same effect, different surface).
- Pristine-tier can't run within 30 days of itself.

The first is fine but worth naming explicitly — the timestamp doesn't disappear from the universe, it relocates from "system-hidden suffix" to "fixture-chosen moniker variability". The operator-facing depot is timestamp-free; the test-facing depot still needs run-uniqueness.

**2. Project ID exact shape.** Two structural options:
- Flat: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}` — e.g., `prlc-alpha`. Short. But you can't tell at a glance whether `prlc-alpha` is a depot, a build artifact, an enshrinement project, etc.
- Typed: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}depot-${MONIKER}` — e.g., `prlc-depot-alpha`. Self-describing. Mirrors the current `${PREFIX}-${TYPE}-${MONIKER}` shape sans timestamp.

Today's `RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT` exists because the same global namespace will host other typed projects (buckets, possibly enshrinement projects). The typed shape preserves that. Worth confirming.

**3. Mason SA and bucket naming consistency.** Today: `mason-${moniker}`, `${PREFIX}-bucket-${moniker}-${ts}`. With the proposal: do these absorb `CLOUD_PREFIX` too, or keep their existing shapes? Consistency argues yes. Length budget might object — service account name max is 30 chars; `${PREFIX}mason-${MONIKER}` could be tight.

**4. The moniker uniqueness contract.** "Levy fails if a depot exists" — but at which layer is that checked?
- GCP API will return 409 on project-id conflict naturally. Levy translates that to a clean failure.
- The current levy already handles 409 idempotently in some places (worker pools at `rbgp_Payor.sh:701-705`). With the new contract, every 409 on derived resources is fatal, no silent retry. That's a posture change worth naming.

## Manifold summary

This is also clearly **not BBAAK scope.** It's a heat of its own — touches RBRR, RBDC, the levy, every derived-name caller, the spec's depot section, marshal-zero blanking discipline, and the operator banner. BBAAK should land cases 4-5 against today's reality (Option 1 from before), and this manifold deserves its own paddock-up before any implementation.

Want me to capture this discussion as an itch with the manifold + open crystals, so it's preserved without losing today's BBAAK thread?

message.content: that 30 day issue is serious.  I'm musing we could make the 'depot list' function report on each existing depot with a fact file per existing depot and the content being whether it is 'active' or 'deleted'.  That way the first step in our prospective cases would be autodetect and autoincrement an id in our test moniker.  ?

content.text:
Yes — that closes the manifold. The autodetect step turns "moniker uniqueness is the operator's problem" into "moniker uniqueness is mechanically managed for any caller that wants it" — which is exactly what the test fixture needs.

## Why this works

GCP's project list already encodes the truth: active projects + soft-deleted projects (visible for 30 days) + nothing-after-30-days. That's the canonical source. Auto-increment isn't tracking a synthetic counter — it's just **reading reality and picking the next free slot**. When GCP eventually releases `pristr01` after 30 days, the next run reuses it naturally. No graveyard accumulation in RBRR, no monotonic drift forever.

The mechanism also has appeal **independent of the RBRR redesign**: even today, an operator running `rbw-dl` benefits from seeing "what monikers are in soft-delete vs active" at a glance. Today's BBAAL recently dropped the moniker column from the listing — but that was when moniker was just a label. If moniker becomes load-bearing identity, the list naturally regrows that column.

## Small design crystals inside the proposal

**Fact emission shape — single multiline file vs N dynamic-basename files.** BBAAJ pattern is one-fact-per-name with const basenames. The list operation is naturally a collection. I'd lean to **one fact with structured multiline content** (e.g., `RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_INVENTORY` with one `moniker status project_id` line per row). Cases read it once, parse it, scan for their prefix family. Avoids dynamic basenames (which feel invented) and avoids the "how do I know how many fact files exist" question. The multiline format mirrors what `rbgp_depot_list` already prints — it's just emitting the same structured data into BURV-readable form.

**Increment scheme.** Two-digit numeric suffix (`pristr01..pristr99`) is plenty: 30-day window × ~once-per-week test runs ≈ <10 active at peak. Bounded width, readable, recyclable. If you ever blow past 99, that's a signal something's wrong with cleanup, not a need for wider counters.

**Status values surfaced.** Existing rbgp_depot_list output uses `delete-requested` as the marker. The fact format should preserve that vocabulary (status = `active` | `delete-requested` | maybe `creating`?). Cases scanning for collision treat anything-not-empty as "in use."

**Precondition vs orchestration.** Where does the autodetect live?
- Inside `rbgp_depot_levy` — levy first lists, picks next free, levies. Operator-friendly. But conflates "list" and "levy" concerns.
- Inside the case — case lists explicitly, computes moniker, then levies with the chosen value. Test-friendly, no levy change.

I'd lean toward keeping autodetect in the case (test-fixture concern), with `rbgp_depot_levy` staying single-purpose. Operator usage stays "operator picks moniker"; test-fixture usage adds a "scan-then-pick" step. Cleanly separable.

## What this means for BBAAK

Two readings:

**Tight reading.** BBAAK works against today's timestamp system. Today, reusing moniker `pristr01` across runs is fine because the timestamp makes each project_id unique; the soft-delete graveyard accumulates inert projects but doesn't block. The autodetect mechanism isn't needed today — it's only the bridge that makes the *future* manifold workable. So BBAAK can ship Option-1 fact emission against today's reality, and this redesign is its own heat.

**Loose reading.** If autodetect-and-increment lands first as a foundational mechanism (independent value: cleaner pristine-lifecycle moniker management even today), then the RBRR redesign rides on top of it. The redesign heat decomposes into: (a) extend depot-list with fact emission + autodetect helper, (b) collapse RBRR fields, (c) absorb prefix into derived names, (d) drop timestamp.

I'd resist binding (a) into BBAAK regardless. Even the loose reading wants (a) as its own pace.

## Where this leaves the manifold

I'd now call it **clear and enactable** with these crystals settled. The remaining design questions (project_id shape, mason/bucket prefix uniformity, 409-fatal posture, suffix width) are small enough to be paddock-bullet decisions in the new heat, not blockers.

Want me to capture both the manifold and the autodetect mechanism as a single itch — preserving the fact that the autodetect is the keystone making the redesign workable, not just an incidental cleanup?

content.text:
Sounds right. A few concerns and one observation worth surfacing before we slate.

## Concerns

**Stale fact files between invocations.** This is the one I'd want to think through. If `rbw-dl` writes `rbgp_fact_depot_pristr100000` on Monday, and by Tuesday the soft-delete has expired and that project is gone from GCP, Tuesday's `rbw-dl` must not leave Monday's stale fact behind. Two postures:

- **Clear-then-write.** First step of `rbgp_depot_list` deletes any prior `rbgp_fact_depot_*` files in the output directory before writing fresh. Each invocation produces a clean snapshot. Simple, robust.
- **BURV-isolated by construction.** Theurge calls go through `rbtdri_invoke_global`, which produces a per-call output directory — so theurge never sees stale files even if the persistent cache had them. But operator-direct `rbw-dl` invocations still need clear-then-write to avoid stale state.

I'd lean clear-then-write at the source. Belt-and-suspenders: theurge's BURV isolation makes it not strictly necessary for the test fixture, but the operator-side hygiene is worth having.

**Cases 2-3 must move to autodetect with this pace, not stay as fixed `pristq01`/`pristg01`.** With timestamp gone, those fixed monikers collide on the second run within 30 days. So the simplification pace touches the BBAAJ-landed cases too — not just data shape, also retrofit cases 2-3 to do the depot-list-then-pick step. Otherwise the fixture self-breaks on second run.

That makes the pace's footprint bigger than just "simplify RBRR" — it's "simplify RBRR + retrofit cases 2-3 + add autodetect-pick helper used by all cases 2-5". BBAAK then becomes small (just cases 4-5 using the helper). Worth being explicit so the docket reflects actual scope.

**The levy's existing idempotent 409 handling (worker pools, `rbgp_Payor.sh:701-705`) becomes a posture decision.** Today the levy treats "already exists" as benign for some sub-resources. With the new fail-fast contract, every 409 on a derived resource is fatal — clean signal of "you collided." That's a conscious change worth naming in the docket so it doesn't drift back accidentally.

## Observation

This pace removes an operator step (manually copying four lines from the levy banner into rbrr.env) and replaces it with autodetect-driven simplicity. That ripples gently into the handbook — the payor onboarding track currently directs the operator to read the banner and edit rbrr.env. That instruction goes away. Probably small (one section of `rbho_payor` or similar), but worth not missing in the spec/handbook update list.

## Direction

I think it's enactable and good. The pace docket should include items 1-12 from the chain we've built up — data shape collapse, derived-name uniformity, cases 2-3 retrofit, autodetect-pick helper, state-value tinder/kindle constants, marshal-zero blanking update, spec amendments, handbook touch-up. BBAAK follows as a much smaller pace.

I'd also keep the "real-time review, no pre-engineered docket" posture for the new pace too — the BBAAK revert lesson applies. Slate it with intent + constraints, not pre-staged choices.

Want me to draft the slate text? Or keep talking through anything still unclear?

content.text:
Yes — clear in intent. Two clarifications I'd want pinned down before it goes into the docket as a contract:

**1. Scope of the wipe.** Does "elements in OUTPUT_DIR" mean *all* contents of `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`, or only files matching a fact-file pattern (e.g., `rbgp_fact_*`)? It matters because OUTPUT_DIR also receives non-fact artifacts — most notably the `.rbra` files written by `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` at `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${instance}.rbra`. If wipe = entire dir, those `.rbra` files are also ephemeral, which means **the operator (or test case) must consume the `.rbra` before invoking any other tabtarget** — that's now an enforced lifecycle, not a politeness. Worth making explicit. (I'd argue wipe-the-whole-dir is right; ephemeral-everything is simpler than two classes of file with different lifecycles. But it does retroactively constrain the install hint flow.)

**2. Who performs the wipe.** Two options:
- **Launcher / workbench-level** (`buw_workbench.sh` or whatever runs after BURD setup) — single point, every tabtarget gets it for free, impossible to forget.
- **Per-tabtarget** — each command author writes the wipe at top.

Launcher-level is the only one I'd accept here. Per-tabtarget guarantees someone forgets, and a forgotten wipe creates phantom fact files that look real to the next caller. Centralizing it in the launcher means the contract is mechanically enforced; individual commands just trust that OUTPUT_DIR is fresh when they enter.

**Suggested phrasing for the docket:**

> Each tabtarget enters with `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` empty. The launcher unconditionally clears `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` before dispatching to the command. All artifacts written to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` during a tabtarget run — fact files, captured artifacts, `.rbra` files — are visible only until the next tabtarget invocation, at which point they are wiped. Callers consume what they need before the next invocation; nothing in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` survives across invocations.

That gives you the cross-invocation invariant + the implementation locus + the implication for `.rbra` consumption, in one statement.

If those two points land your way, then yes, the lifecycle is clear enough to commit to.

Proposed docket (intent-only per BBAAK-revert lesson — short, no pre-staged choices):

```
## Character

Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
hidden behind a timestamp.

Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.

## Scope

- Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
  (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
- Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
  replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
- Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
  BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
  State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
- Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
  via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
  Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
  prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
- Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
- RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
  bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
  are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
  the next tabtarget.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
  beyond intent.
- BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).

## Out of scope

- BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
- Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
  banner-copy instruction.
- Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
  a pre-locked redesign.
- 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
  at implementation.
```
